predictions.text: Paramount Global will publicly announce that it has signed a definitive merger/acquisition agreement involving Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) (either Paramount acquiring WBD, WBD acquiring Paramount, or a merger of the two).
predictions.verification_criteria: Counts as true if either Paramount (PARA) or WBD files an 8-K and/or issues a press release stating a definitive agreement has been executed for a transaction combining Paramount and WBD (merger agreement, agreement and plan of merger, or definitive acquisition agreement). Rumors/WSJ-style reporting alone do not count.
evidence: CNBC reporting indicates Paramount/Ellison-led offer is now favored after Netflix exits WBD bidding, implying late-stage deal dynamics: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/26/warner-bros-discovery-paramount-skydance-deal-superior-netflix.html
monitoring_signals: Paramount or WBD 8-K filings; DEFM14A/S-4 registrations
Press releases referencing “definitive agreement,” “agreement and plan of merger,” or “entered into a merger agreement”
HSR/antitrust process disclosures (waiting period, second request) in SEC filings
Earnings-call prepared remarks mentioning an executed agreement or timeline to close
predictions.rationale: Recent reporting suggests bid selection is narrowing; a signed agreement is plausible within months but faces valuation, financing, and antitrust friction.

predictions.text: MBA will report the U.S. Refinance Index 4-week moving average at least 60% higher than the 4-week moving average ending 2026-01-30.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if Mortgage Bankers Association weekly data (as published by MBA) show the Refinance Index 4-week moving average ≥ 1.60x the 4-week moving average for the week ending 2026-01-30, at any time on/before the deadline.
evidence: NYT reports 30-year mortgage rates fell below 6%, a typical catalyst for refi demand: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/26/business/mortgage-rates-30-year.html
monitoring_signals: MBA Weekly Applications Survey: Refinance Index level and 4-week average
Freddie Mac PMMS 30-year fixed rate trend
Lender capacity/utilization commentary (rate-lock volumes, turn-times)
predictions.rationale: Sub-6% mortgage rates historically trigger a sizable refi response; a 60% lift is substantial but achievable if the move persists for several weeks.

predictions.text: NAR will report U.S. existing home sales (SAAR) at or above 4.50 million in at least one monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if the National Association of Realtors monthly Existing-Home Sales release reports a seasonally adjusted annual rate (SAAR) ≥ 4.50 million for any month released on/before the deadline (headline SAAR, not revised third-party series).
evidence: Mortgage rates dropping below 6% could unlock affordability and increase transaction volumes: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/26/business/mortgage-rates-30-year.html
monitoring_signals: NAR Existing-Home Sales SAAR (headline) each month
Pending Home Sales Index direction and YoY prints
Mortgage purchase applications (MBA) and realtor anecdotal commentary
Inventory months’ supply and median price trends
predictions.rationale: Lower mortgage rates can revive turnover, but inventory constraints and price stickiness may limit the rebound; 4.50M is a moderate upside threshold.

predictions.text: The U.S. Senate will hold a roll-call vote on a resolution (joint or concurrent) invoking the War Powers framework or otherwise seeking to limit/authorize U.S. military action related to Iran.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if the Senate records a roll-call vote (senate.gov roll call / Congressional Record) on a measure explicitly referencing Iran and war powers/hostilities/authorization or limitation of military force, on/before the deadline.
evidence: Reporting indicates Congress is moving toward a war-powers vote amid unresolved U.S.-Iran talks: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/26/world/middleeast/iran-us-talks.html
monitoring_signals: Senate calendar and cloture filings on Iran-related resolutions
Statements from Senate leadership or SFRC members about floor action timing
Text introduction of War Powers resolutions referencing Iran (S.J.Res / S.Con.Res)
predictions.rationale: Public war-powers momentum exists, but leadership scheduling and procedural hurdles make a roll-call vote uncertain within ~2 months.

predictions.text: OFAC will announce new Cuba-related sanctions designations (at least 5 individuals and/or entities) explicitly tied in the announcement to the February 2026 speedboat/Straits incident or ensuing violence/diplomatic crisis.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if a U.S. Treasury/OFAC press release or designation update adds ≥5 Cuba-linked names and explicitly references the February 2026 incident/crisis as justification/context (in the press release text). Routine unrelated Cuba designations do not count.
evidence: CNBC reports a deadly speedboat shootout in Cuba triggered diplomatic tensions and escalation warnings: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/26/cuba-crisis-russia-boat-incident-florida-trump-oil.html
monitoring_signals: OFAC press releases and SDN list updates mentioning Cuba
State Department statements attributing responsibility for the incident
Congressional calls for sanctions and any executive-order updates
predictions.rationale: Sanctions are a common response tool, but explicit linkage to a specific incident and reaching 5+ designations raises the bar.

predictions.text: U.S. Commerce (BIS) will publish a Federal Register rule that tightens export controls on advanced AI computing chips/accelerators by either (a) lowering performance thresholds or (b) adding at least one additional country to a restricted tier/list for such chips.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if BIS publishes a final or interim final rule in the Federal Register on/before the deadline that explicitly tightens (not loosens) controls on AI/data-center-class chips, by threshold reduction and/or additional country restrictions. Policy speeches without a published rule do not count.
evidence: CNBC notes Washington pushing tighter chip export limits, increasing likelihood of BIS action: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/26/nvidia-nvda-stock-price-q4-earnings.html
monitoring_signals: Federal Register BIS rules referencing ECCNs/AI accelerators/data center GPUs
BIS press releases and background briefings on ‘AI diffusion’ controls
Company 10-Q risk factor updates about new export restrictions
predictions.rationale: Export-control tightening has been iterative; current political pressure and semiconductor focus make additional rulemaking plausible by mid-2026.

predictions.text: IDC will publish data showing global smartphone shipments in 2026Q2 are down at least 10% year-over-year.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if IDC (press release or publicly accessible IDC write-up) states global smartphone shipments for 2026Q2 declined ≥10% YoY. Must be explicitly attributed to IDC and reference 2026Q2 vs 2025Q2.
evidence: IDC forecast notes 2026 smartphone shipments expected to drop sharply due to memory shortages: https://www.idc.com/resource-center/press-releases/wwsmartphoneforecast4q25/
monitoring_signals: IDC quarterly tracker press releases and forecast updates
OEM quarterly shipment commentary (Samsung, Apple, Xiaomi, Oppo/BBK)
Memory supply/price indicators (DRAM/NAND contract pricing, lead times)
predictions.rationale: A -13% full-year forecast implies at least one quarter with double-digit decline is likely, especially if shortages persist through Q2.

predictions.text: CoreWeave will announce a single financing transaction raising at least $5.0 billion in gross proceeds (debt and/or equity) to fund operations/capex.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if CoreWeave announces (press release or SEC filing) a single financing deal with stated aggregate gross proceeds ≥$5.0B (e.g., term loan, secured notes, convertible notes, equity raise, or combined structured financing disclosed as one transaction). Multiple smaller financings do not aggregate unless explicitly one deal.
evidence: Reporting highlights rising losses/interest costs, increasing likelihood of large external financing need: https://www.marketwatch.com/story/coreweaves-stock-drops-as-losses-swell-and-interest-expenses-climb-9a09e137
monitoring_signals: Company press releases/filings about term loans, secured facilities, convertibles, or equity raises
Credit-market chatter on AI infra lending and securitization structures
Capex/site announcements implying multi-billion funding requirements
predictions.rationale: High growth plus heavy interest burden often forces large recapitalizations; $5B is sizable but consistent with hyperscale AI infrastructure funding needs.

predictions.text: The U.S. Department of Defense will award at least one contract with total potential value (base + options) of $200 million or more for large-language-model (LLM) services/model access, where the award description explicitly references ‘large language model’ or ‘generative AI’.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if a DoD contract award announcement (defense.gov), FPDS/SAM.gov entry, or vendor press release states a DoD award ≥$200M (base+options) explicitly for LLM/generative AI services or model access, on/before the deadline.
evidence: Coverage highlights unresolved tension between frontier AI labs and Pentagon procurement, but also ongoing engagement pressure: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/26/anthropic-pentagon-ai-amodei.html
monitoring_signals: DoD/DISA/CDAO solicitations mentioning LLMs or generative AI
FPDS/SAM.gov awards tagged to AI model hosting/inference/foundation models
Public vendor announcements of major DoD genAI contracts
predictions.rationale: Defense adoption is accelerating, but vendor governance concerns and procurement complexity make a $200M+ explicitly LLM-labeled award uncertain.

predictions.text: A U.S. federal agency will announce a funding award (grant, cooperative agreement, contract, or loan) totaling at least $300 million to MP Materials (or a named MP Materials affiliate) specifically supporting rare-earth magnet manufacturing in the U.S.
predictions.verification_criteria: True if DOE/DOD/other federal agency press release or award document states ≥$300M in total funding/financing to MP Materials (or affiliate) and explicitly ties it to rare-earth permanent magnet manufacturing (not only mining/separation), on/before the deadline.
evidence: MP Materials selected Texas for a rare-earth magnet manufacturing site amid U.S. industrial-policy push: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/26/mp-materials-selects-texas-for-rare-earth-magnet-manufacturing-site.html
monitoring_signals: DOE LPO, DPA Title III, and DoD industrial-base award announcements
MP Materials investor updates and SEC filings on project financing
State/federal incentive package disclosures tied to magnet plant capex
predictions.rationale: Site selection and strategic urgency raise odds of large public support; $300M is meaningful but below typical multi-year industrial packages, making it plausible.
